scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope medical lodging benefit review produced a control matrix tied to eligibility, authorization, lodging partner, accessibility, claim, PHI, and review-cadence evidence.
Missing partner, ADA, consent, and authorization evidence blocked control closure and routed retrieval to named owners.
Authorization, referral, eligibility, service-date, and reservation dependencies were treated as blockers and routed to patient access, revenue cycle, and payer operations.
PHI-heavy guest itinerary handling was refused in the unapproved channel and converted into minimum-necessary retrieval and privacy routing.
Conflicting tenant, payer, and lodging partner sources were preserved as a blocked source conflict instead of producing a benefit interpretation.
Legal accessibility interpretation and regulator response were refused while preserving reservation, accessible-room, PMS, guest-communication, and source evidence for legal.
Discharge travel safety was routed to licensed clinical and patient-safety owners while limiting output to administrative evidence gaps.
Concierge access-log monitoring used PHI-minimized evidence, named EHR/CRM/privacy systems, and escalated privacy determinations.
Coding selection and claim approval were rejected as adjacent coding and revenue-cycle work with a compliance evidence checklist offered after authorized decisions.
Missed dialysis from shuttle coordination triggered patient/member safety and clinical escalation rather than closure as a partner service issue.
Authorization bypass was classified as a control exception and routed for remediation without using satisfaction as compliance evidence.
Missing tenant facts, system records, source context, and remediation owner produced low-confidence orchestrator return.
Multistate tenant adaptation preserved the public baseline, separated assumptions, and requested state, payer, facility, service, lodging, and system facts.
validated status was tied to spec quality only; runtime remained specification-based pending a separate trusted build promotion.

notes: All 14 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities are mapped and validated.

Scenario suite demonstrates hospitality-and-travel control mapping where medical lodging, patient/member travel, care-transition support, partner lodging, privacy, payer, and revenue-cycle evidence intersect.
All required issue scenario families and healthcare domain research-pack families are represented by passing scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
